vllm.title: [Core] Registry for processing model inputs
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR builds on the multimodal registry (MultiModalRegistry) introduced by #4197, which acts at the ModelRunner level to map multimodal inputs to a dictionary of input tensors. In particular, a new registry (InputRegistry) is introduced to process the tokenized inputs at the LLMEngine level.

vllm.title: [Spec Decode] Introduce DraftModelRunner
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR introduces a DraftModelRunner for speculative decoding. The unique feature of DraftModelRunner is allowing multiple steps in one execute_model(). In this way, the following features can be implemented inside this runner in parallel without affecting non-speculative decoding use cases:

Bonus

vllm.title: [ Bugfix ] Enabling Loading Models With Fused QKV/MLP on Disk with FP8
vllm.author: robertgshaw2-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
SUMMARY:

some models have fused q,k,v and fused mlp on disk (e.g. Phi3)
our fp8 implementation assumes there is a separate key for each q,k,v during weight loading. Because our kernels require a single scale (torch.scaled_mm), we load 3 scales for QKVColumnLinear. Then, after the weights have been

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Only add `Attention.kv_scale` if kv cache quantization is enabled
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR addresses an issue where Attention.kv_scale parameters were being created unnecessarily for models without FP8 KV cache quantization.
Now we will only create and attempt to load kv_scale parameters when FP8 KV cache quantization is explicitly enabled with kv_cache_dtype. This change prevents

vllm.title: [Kernel] Flashinfer for prefill & decode, with Cudagraph support for decode
vllm.author: LiuXiaoxuanPKU
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is the second PR to integrate flashinfer. The goal is to use flashinfer for the prefill phase (including prefix caching). Hopefully, we can get rid of the context_attention_fwd triton kernel and use flashinfer append kernel for better performance.
The PR also tries to add the cuda graph support

vllm.title: [Bugfix][TPU] Fix TPU sampler output
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR fixes a bug introduced in #5799.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][TPU] Fix pad slot id
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR changes the TPU backend's _PAD_SLOT_ID from 0 to -1. The constant is used to discard padding tokens when inserting the attention KV into the KV cache. In PyTorch XLA, it seems the index -1 can be used with the index_copy_ API to let XLA ignore the corresponding slice of tensors.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] fix missing last itl in openai completions benchmark
vllm.author: mcalman
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Previously, an itl (inter-token latency) wasn't being recorded if the "usage" field was present. This would often ignore the itl for the last token because the API returns the "usage" field along with the last token. The already present if statement (if data["choices"][0]["text"]:) should suffice to

vllm.title: [Misc] Extend vLLM Metrics logging API
vllm.author: SolitaryThinker
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently, vLLM handles all of its metric logging in a single StatsLogger class, which has a hardcoded Prometheus client and text output. We would like to support our own Prometheus client and other arbitrary loggers.
Solution (Thanks to @Yard1)


Clean up StatLogger API by introducing a base class
